mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, the Federal Reserve maintains a restrictive monetary stance that keeps real rates elevated. This suppresses equity valuations and increases bond yields as investors demand higher compensation for duration risk. The 10-year Treasury yield rises in response to sustained policy tightness.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls on average when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% in more than 40% of triggered 20-day windows, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS (month-over-month growth > 0.2%) signals robust labor demand and wage pressure, increasing inflation expectations. Higher inflation expectations push the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand inflation risk premium.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines when PAYEMS growth is positive in more than 50% of 20-day forward periods, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and yield changes is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) both decline simultaneously, aggregate demand weakens. This signals recession risk, causing investors to flee to safe-haven Treasuries. The 10-year Treasury yield falls as demand for duration increases and growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint decline signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: INDPRO (industrial production) growth above 0.3% month-over-month indicates supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This reduces inflation pressure and allows the Fed to maintain accommodative policy, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield lower as growth-adjusted real rates decline.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.3% month-over-month sustained for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of triggered 60-day windows, or Rank IC between INDPRO growth and yield changes is negative or below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL (headline CPI) accelerating above 3.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS below 3.5% creates a negative real rate environment. This inflation-adjusted tightness forces the Fed to signal future rate hikes, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the market prices in higher terminal rates.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE rising above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced wage growth expectations. Lower wage pressure reduces inflation expectations, allowing the Fed to cut rates. The 10-year Treasury yield falls as investors price in lower future short rates and reduced inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of triggered 20-day windows, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and yield direction is positive or below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 (real GDP) growth accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals strong economic expansion. This increases inflation expectations and reduces the probability of Fed rate cuts, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher as investors demand higher compensation for duration in a strong growth environment.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of triggered 60-day windows, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and yield changes is negative or below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST (housing starts) falls below 1.2 million units annualized while RSAFS (retail sales) remains flat or declining, residential investment and consumer spending both weaken. This dual demand shock signals recession risk, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall sharply as investors rotate into safe-haven duration.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.2M annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month change ≤ 0%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 60% of triggered 5-day windows, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
